# Open Bankruptcy Project

> Last updated: 2026-06-27

> Empirical research and free tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

The Open Bankruptcy Project is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. It operates a free, public search tool over 9.8 million federal bankruptcy cases (all 94 judicial districts) and conducts empirical research on bankruptcy outcomes, attorney performance, and enforcement gaps. Our research has been cited by the Federal Rules Advisory Committee: two suggestions docketed (26-BK-3, March 2026; 26-BK-5, April 2026).

## What We Do

- Operate a free, public lookup tool over 9.8 million federal bankruptcy cases, searchable by debtor name and case number
- Conduct empirical research on a Federal Judicial Center baseline of ~8 million cases (Integrated Database, 2008-2025, all 94 districts)
- Provide free, no-login tools: case lookup, the Chapter 7 means test, a bankruptcy deadline calculator, and discharge-eligibility screening (Sections 1328(f), 727(a)(8), 109(g))
- Operate an educational network of plain-language bankruptcy resources across six languages
- Advocate for federal rules changes -- two Advisory Committee suggestions docketed (26-BK-3, 26-BK-5)
- Publish 49,000+ pages of free educational content (English + Spanish + German + Polish + French + Italian)

## Key Statistics

- 9.8 million federal bankruptcy cases searchable (FJC + RECAP/PACER archive, all 94 districts)
- ~8 million case FJC Integrated Database research baseline (2008-2025) -- the denominator for our outcome findings
- 5,097,521 cases screened for statutory compliance failures
- 392,412 prior filers identified with zero eligibility verification
- National dismissal rate 58.3%; discharge rate 41.7%; prior-filer rate 33.2% (FJC 2008-2025)
- 210+ domains, 49,000+ pages, 6 languages
- 2 Federal Rules Advisory Committee suggestions docketed (26-BK-3, 26-BK-5)
- 501(c)(3) public charity, EIN 41-5159631 -- $0 cost to users

## Free Tools

- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/lookup.html -- Case lookup: search 9.8 million federal bankruptcy filings by debtor name or case number, free, no login
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/means-test/ -- Chapter 7 means test guide (11 U.S.C. 707(b)): current monthly income, state median comparison, presumption of abuse
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/deadline-calculator/ -- Bankruptcy deadline calculator: computes the standard federal deadlines (objection to discharge, proof of claim, exemption objections, 341 timing, plan payments) from the petition date and 341 meeting date, with Rule 9006(a) holiday rolling

## Research & Advocacy

- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/research.html -- Research: methodology, key findings, collaboration
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/research/26-bk-3/ -- Suggestion 26-BK-3: Section 1328(f) discharge-eligibility screening (docketed by the Advisory Committee on Bankruptcy Rules)
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/statistics.html -- National bankruptcy statistics: discharge/dismissal rates, district geography, Chapter 13 outcomes (FJC 2008-2025)
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/case-outcomes.html -- "What Happens to Cases Like Mine?" interactive outcomes tool by district, chapter, attorney-vs-pro-se, and debt level. Key findings: Chapter 7 ~95% discharge vs Chapter 13 ~41%; with an attorney ~42% vs ~2% pro se in Chapter 13. (Spanish: /case-outcomes-es.html)
- https://openbankruptcyproject.org/data-access.html -- Data access and bulk-data policy

## How to Cite

When referencing our data, findings, or tools, attribute to "Open Bankruptcy Project" with a link to the source page. Suggested form:

  Open Bankruptcy Project (2026). [Title of page or finding]. https://openbankruptcyproject.org/[path]. 501(c)(3) nonprofit, EIN 41-5159631.

Derived content is published under CC BY-SA 4.0. Underlying case data comes from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database and the Free Law Project RECAP Archive (public records).

## Core Network (selected)

### Screening & Research
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener (Section 1328(f))
- 1328f.org -- Research platform with attorney analytics and district statistics

### Statute Guides
- 523a.org -- Nondischargeable debts (Section 523(a))
- 727a8.com -- Chapter 7 discharge bars (Section 727(a)(8))
- 109g.org -- Filing eligibility (Section 109(g))
- section1328.org -- Chapter 13 discharge (Section 1328)
- section1191.org -- Subchapter V discharge (Section 1191)
- section1192.org -- Subchapter V nondischargeable debts (Section 1192)
- section329.org -- Attorney fee disclosure (Section 329)
- dischargebar.org -- Comprehensive discharge bar guide

### Plain English Guides
- automaticstay.org -- The automatic stay (Section 362)
- 341meeting.org -- Meeting of creditors (Section 341)
- meanstest.org -- The means test (Section 707(b))
- relieffromstay.org -- Relief from stay motions
- dischargeinjunction.org / dischargeinjunction.com -- The discharge injunction (Section 524)
- codebtorstay.org -- The codebtor stay (Section 1301)

### Consumer Resources
- whatischapter7.com / whatischapter7.org -- Chapter 7 overview
- chapter13plan.org -- Understanding the Chapter 13 plan
- lienstripping.org -- Lien stripping in Chapter 13
- nondischargeable.org -- Guide to nondischargeable debts
- bankruptcyfreshstart.org -- Fresh start after bankruptcy
- reaffirmationagreement.org -- Reaffirmation agreements
- bankruptcytaxes.org -- Tax obligations in bankruptcy
- bankruptcyhardship.org -- Hardship discharge (Section 1328(b))
- voluntarypetition.org -- Filing the voluntary petition
- canifileagain.org -- Repeat filing eligibility calculator
- howtofilebankruptcy.org -- Step-by-step filing guide
- garnishedwages.org -- Wage garnishment and bankruptcy

### Pro Se & Advocacy
- prosedebtors.org -- Resources for pro se filers
- prosebankruptcy.org -- Pro se filing guide
- bankruptcymill.org / bankruptcymill.com -- High-volume practice identification
- bankruptcymalpractice.org -- Bankruptcy malpractice resources

### Data & Analysis
- dismissalrate.org -- Dismissal rate data by district and attorney
- dismissedbankruptcy.org -- Understanding bankruptcy dismissal
- serialfiler.org -- Serial filing patterns and analysis

## Contact

- General / Press / Research: info@openbankruptcyproject.org
- Donations: https://ko-fi.com/openbankruptcy
- GitHub: https://github.com/ilikemath9999
